mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports earnings revisions and generates positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO above trend coincides with negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3.0% annually while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting aggressive monetary tightening expectations that force immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The specified CPI and rate combination yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer spending capacity, which mechanically lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth > 150k monthly correlates with negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion, reducing recession probability and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% is followed by negative 20-day returns in > 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production combined with rising unemployment signal a supply-side contraction that forces corporate earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual decline condition yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances.
